codeReview.summary: The feature correctly wires the Refresh affordance to `handleRuntimeCommand({ type: "refresh_all_peers" })` and adds an inline offline-peer error indicator, but it does not implement the required displayed `last_seen` progression behavior for peer rows.

issues.description: VAL-OPS-010/011 require the peer row's displayed `last_seen` to advance after refresh completion for online peers, but this row still renders only `"Ready"` (or static `paperLatency(...)`) and never renders `peer.last_seen`. As implemented, users cannot observe `last_seen` progression after `refresh_all_peers`, so the feature's stated expected behavior is not fully met.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-worker skill requires `demo-gallery` Playwright to pass unconditionally, but mission AGENTS documents a known pre-existing failing gallery case (`dashboard-running` nested button warning) that workers are explicitly told not to fix in unrelated features. This creates contradictory guidance during verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/runtime-worker/SKILL.md:42-47 requires gallery pass; mission AGENTS.md:91-95 records the known pre-existing failure; handoff shows `npx playwright test ...demo-gallery...` exitCode 1 (handoff lines 31-34) despite successful feature completion.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS says known pre-existing issues should not be re-reported in handoff `discoveredIssues`, but this handoff still includes the tracked nested-button issue. This suggests the convention is not sufficiently reinforced in worker procedure/checklists.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md:93-95 says 'NOT re-report ... in discoveredIssues'; handoff lines 76-81 re-list the same nested-button issue.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton, handoff, skill, and commit 86dc17c. Found a blocking requirement gap: peer-row displayed `last_seen` progression is still not implemented. Also noted shared-state guidance conflicts around expected gallery pass behavior and known-issue re-reporting.
